title: Quick check: finding the in-order successor

questions.text: In the original tree, node 10 has two children: 5 (a leaf) and 15 (a leaf). If you call delete(root, 10), what value is the in-order successor used to replace 10?
questions.explanation: The in-order successor is the smallest value in the right subtree: start at 10's right child, 15, and keep going left. 15 has no left child, so it is the smallest value there already, and it becomes the successor.

questions.text: To find the in-order successor of a node you are deleting, which node do you use?
choices: The leftmost node in the node's right subtree
The rightmost node in the node's left subtree
questions.explanation: The leftmost node in the right subtree is correct: it is always guaranteed to have no left child of its own, so removing it never creates a second two-children case. Picking the direct right child (option 1) only happens to be correct when that child has no left child of its own; in general you must keep walking left. The rightmost node in the left subtree (option 3) is the in-order predecessor, a different but also valid node that this lesson does not use.
